---
name: Benchmark feedback / methodology question
about: Question or critique of RHOB's methodology, numbers, or design -- not tied to a specific detector or family submission
title: ""
labels: feedback
assignees: ''
---

## What seems off, unclear, or worth discussing?

Be as specific as possible: a number that looks surprising, an assumption you
think doesn't hold, a comparison you'd expect to see but don't. If you can
point to the exact figure/table/file, even better.

## Why does it matter?

What would change (in the benchmark, in how it should be interpreted, or in
your own research) depending on the answer?

## Have you tried to reproduce or investigate it yourself?

If so, what did you find? See [REPRODUCIBILITY.md](https://github.com/Aarav500/rhob/blob/main/REPRODUCIBILITY.md)
for how the experiments are meant to be regenerated -- several real bugs in
RHOB were found by external reviewers questioning a number and us tracing it
to a root cause rather than adjusting it to look better. We'd rather hear
"this looks wrong, why?" than have it go unreported.
